action.reasoning: Observation: Current best test PESQ=3.137 (with -25 LUFS norm), val PESQ=2.862 on 100 samples. Loudness sweep was completely flat (2.9134) from -35 to -23 on 30 samples. Analysis: Fixed loudness target works but may be suboptimal since files vary in input level/SNR (0-15dB); matching enhanced loudness to each noisy input's measured loudness could improve consistency and PESQ (common SE heuristic). Root cause of flat sweep likely because PESQ evaluator is robust in broad range but per-file adaptation might still help. Decision: Extend test_enhance.py with new enhance_and_match_noisy_loudness func + evaluation on 100 val samples (incl. mean noisy LUFS); if val PESQ >2.862 then adopt in predict.py next (dynamic per-file target from noisy). No training yet as baseline strong; measure if this post-process beats fixed norm before trying ConvTasNet.
